title: Request Deduplication & Race Conditions
description:
Build a search interface that properly handles concurrent requests, deduplicates in-flight requests, and prevents race conditions where stale responses overwrite fresh data.

### Requirements

- A search input triggers API calls as the user types (debounced 300ms)
- If a new request fires while a previous one is still in-flight, abort the previous request
- Track and display all in-flight requests with their status
- Use AbortController to cancel stale requests
- Only apply the result from the most recent request (ignore stale responses)
- Show a request log displaying: request ID, query, status, duration
- Implement request deduplication: if the same query is requested again within 5s, return cached result
- Show cache hit/miss indicator

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Search input | `search-input` |
| Results list | `results-list` |
| Result item | `result-{index}` |
| Loading indicator | `loading` |
| Request log | `request-log` |
| Log entry | `log-{index}` |
| Cache indicator | `cache-indicator` |
| Active requests | `active-requests` |
constraints:
- Must use AbortController to cancel stale in-flight requests
- Must debounce at 300ms
- Must ignore responses that arrive after a newer request was sent
- Must cache results for 5 seconds
- Must display request lifecycle in the log
- Race condition: if response B arrives after response A was requested later, B's result must be discarded
hints: Maintain a `requestIdRef` that increments with each request. Before applying results, check if the current request ID matches the latest one.
Use a `Map<string, { data: any; timestamp: number }>` for the dedup cache. Before fetching, check if a cached entry exists and is less than 5s old.
Abort the previous request's controller before creating a new one. Store the controller in a ref.
